utils.functions

Shared utility functions used across all blueprints.

Attributes

DB_CONFIG

Functions

get_db_connection()

Open and return a connection to the MySQL database on AWS RDS.

to_minutes(t)

Convert a time value to total minutes since midnight.

has_time_conflict(current_schedule, new_times)

Determine whether a set of new course times conflicts with an existing schedule.

Module Contents

utils.functions.DB_CONFIG[source]
utils.functions.get_db_connection()[source]

Open and return a connection to the MySQL database on AWS RDS.

Uses DictCursor so columns are accessible by name, matching the sqlite3.Row behaviour used throughout the application.

Returns:

An open database connection.

Return type:

pymysql.connections.Connection

utils.functions.to_minutes(t)[source]

Convert a time value to total minutes since midnight.

Accepts either a datetime.timedelta (as returned by PyMySQL for TIME columns) or a 'HH:MM' / 'HH:MM:SS' string.

Parameters:

t (timedelta | str) – The time value to convert.

Returns:

Total minutes since midnight.

Return type:

int

utils.functions.has_time_conflict(current_schedule, new_times)[source]

Determine whether a set of new course times conflicts with an existing schedule.

Two time slots conflict if they share the same day and do not have at least a 30-minute gap between them.

Parameters:

  • current_schedule (list[dict]) – Dicts containing ‘day’, ‘start_time’, and ‘end_time’ for each currently enrolled course.

  • new_times (list[dict]) – Dicts with the same keys for the course being evaluated.

Returns:

True if a conflict is detected, False otherwise.

Return type:

bool
